title: stachys serpentina
intro: Stachys serpentina, also known as Indian hedgemaze or wood mint, is a perennial plant in the mint family (Lamiaceae) native to the Himalayas and the Indian subcontinent. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to the presence of alkaloids such as vasicine and vasicinone. The health benefits of Stachys serpentina include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use as an anti-ulcer agent and a remedy for fever and inflammation. Stachys serpentina is often prepared as a herbal tea, capsule, or tincture, and is also used in combination with other herbs in traditional Ayurvedic and Unani medicine.
